characterCraft.note: Redrawn at reference scale from the old 27x28 lead / 19px passengers: the lead now measures 9x11 drawn (Machine Hunt hunter ~10, under the Block Mine 12-13 hero ceiling) with a constructed silhouette — shaded body over dark underlayer, belly, eye bumps with pupils and facing, tucked haunches, gold crown and swaying scarf. Passengers are 7x7..7x9 with per-style palettes and 1px accessories (bow, cap, spots, leaf, tadpole tail); pickups are 8x12 waving kids. Character reads from the hop cycle: crouch anticipation, mid-air leg-kick stretch with shadow separation, landing squash, idle breathing, and alarm-wide eyes.

environmentCraft.note: As the actors shrank the world was densified, all render-layer: tire-wear dashes, cracks, and blinking reflector posts on the road; pebbles and wildflowers on the shoulders; drifting glints, bow ripples off every log, and hovering dragonflies on the river; butterflies over the meadow; fireflies in the late-season nursery. The banded sky, seasonal landmarks (garden, mill wheel, covered bridge, drowned town), bridge piers, bus shelter, reeds, and foreground occlusion band all remain, and the HUD-free world still reads place and mood.

levelVariety.note: Same-state season captures prove structural progression on the new art: measured structure distances .099 spring/summer, .148 spring/autumn, .167 summer/autumn, and .306..316 for every flood pair. Spring keeps the garden fence and three-lane road; Midsummer builds the dock, sluice, and working wheel; Autumn replaces the first river silhouette with a covered bridge; Flood Season drowns the median and upper lane, adds refuge islets, skiffs, and submerged signs.

animationImpact.note: Aligned real-pixel bursts on the small frogs still measure strongly (lead hop burst max .583, passenger .512): crouch, arc, kick, and squash survive at 160x360. The flood warning transforms the world before landfall (delta .436), the sacrifice leaps bodily with a trailing gold streak and on-arrival impact rays (delta .157, burst .124), and the whole-family apex holds exact hitstop, slow stepping, a real admire pause, and a broad gold homecoming (delta .939).

readability.note: Bright cream/lime/cyan/gold frogs keep contrast against water, bark, and asphalt at native size, and the smaller actors give hazards visibly more room: threats cross 76% of their wrap track on screen and the sampled actor footprint is 6.6-9.6% of the playfield. Vehicle gaps, log cracks, the flood chevrons, refuge beacon, family pips, tactic verb, and loss/home totals all remain legible; intent reads from the leader's crouch and facing with no drawn route overlay.

artDirectionCohesion.note: One storybook-marsh grammar at a single consistent scale: rounded shaded frog anatomy, bark rings and end caps on the slimmer logs, top-down cab/window/wheel vehicle construction at the Pocket League 14px reference (car 15, truck/bus 23), the 32x17 croc set-piece, marsh greens against water cyans, gold reward and coral danger language. The density pass extends the existing material triples instead of pasting noise on top.

guidelineOverlays: none: the former dashed route-cue arc and target reticle on the leader's next hop were removed as banned guideline overlays; the family ribbon links only the CURRENT bodies of live convoy members, and the sacrifice streak trails the leaping ghost's already-flown path with the impact ring appearing only on arrival. No path lines, predicted arcs, or future-position reticles are drawn at any sampled beat.
